jokes.text: Is your dog friendly - or just ugly? - Very friendly! But, he's not a dog.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if a dog is friendly or ugly, expecting a typical pet owner response
jokes.punchline: The owner confirms it's friendly but reveals it's not actually a dog
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection with good economy - sets up expectation and subverts it cleanly

jokes.text: Tonight's winning number was approximately six.
jokes.setup: Formal announcement of a winning number
jokes.punchline: The precision of 'approximately six' is absurdly vague for a lottery
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor that plays with the contradiction between precision and approximation

jokes.text: Don't most pets eat at home and poop outside, like college students?
jokes.setup: Question about normal pet behavior
jokes.punchline: Comparing pets to college students' habits
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy that feels like something Fry would say, with nice parallel structure

jokes.text: So, that's why you never clean it! Uh, right.
jokes.setup: Elaborate explanation about special alien litter
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes Leela just makes excuses to avoid cleaning, Leela awkwardly confirms
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bender cutting through BS and Leela's embarrassed deflection feels very true to both characters

jokes.text: I don't do subtitles. When I go to the movies, I don't wanna have to look up from my phone.
jokes.setup: Bender declining to see a foreign film
jokes.punchline: His reason is that subtitles would interfere with phone use during movies
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - perfectly captures modern movie-going rudeness while being very Bender. Sharp social observation with great delivery

jokes.text: Nibbler! You can talk! You always forget I can talk. Nibbler! You can talk!
jokes.setup: Leela expresses surprise that Nibbler can talk
jokes.punchline: Nibbler points out she always forgets, then she immediately does it again
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of a classic running gag with immediate payoff - the repetition is the joke itself

jokes.text: Although I wasn't crazy about that big floating head. It was the best thing in the movie! No, no. The one in front of me, blocking my view.
jokes.setup: Bender complains about a big floating head, Leela defends it
jokes.punchline: Bender clarifies he means an actual person's head blocking his view, not something in the movie
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with movie theater experience - audience assumes he's talking about the film

jokes.text: What's a 2x3x5 letter name for a 3x23x29 number name for a movie featuring a 1x4x9 object? Easy. A Space Odyssey.
jokes.setup: Incredibly complex mathematical crossword clue
jokes.punchline: Fry solves it instantly with the obvious answer '2001: A Space Odyssey'
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical wordplay that works on multiple levels - the complexity masks a simple answer, and it's funny that Fry gets it

jokes.text: Nibbler, you really are my BFF. My best furry friend. Too corny?
jokes.setup: Leela makes a sweet comment to Nibbler
jokes.punchline: She immediately second-guesses herself about being too corny
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Leela's self-consciousness about sentiment - feels realistic but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Merely a hairball. I'm perfectly... [collapses]
jokes.setup: Nibbler dismisses his symptoms as minor
jokes.punchline: He immediately collapses mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic timing - the collapse immediately contradicts his claim of being fine

jokes.text: Without treatment, he'll be left with the IQ of a terrier. A Boston terrier.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg warns Nibbler will have the IQ of a terrier
jokes.punchline: He clarifies it's specifically a Boston terrier (implying they're dumber)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that plays on dog breed stereotypes - the specificity makes it funnier

jokes.text: What is it... Sorry. Ivermectin. It's a powerful dewormer, okay? Now, if you're done interrupting, we're finished. Thank you... I! Wasn't! Finished!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to explain the cure but keeps getting interrupted
jokes.punchline: He explodes in frustration when thanked, insisting he wasn't done
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Zoidberg - his frustration at being interrupted feels very authentic and builds nicely

jokes.text: No. That's not all. Let me finish speaking for once. [continues] I! Wasn't! Finished!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to deliver medical information but keeps getting interrupted
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frustrated outbursts about not being allowed to finish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - his desperate need for respect and tendency to get flustered

jokes.text: My mom always hid my medicine in something yummy. Like paste. That's sad and dumb.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests hiding medicine in something tasty
jokes.punchline: His example is paste, revealing his poor childhood, Leela's blunt response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - reveals his sad backstory casually, and Leela's reaction is perfectly blunt and unsympathetic

jokes.text: I love that game! You know, there's a trick to winning. Always be X.
jokes.setup: Fry gets excited about tic-tac-toe
jokes.punchline: His 'strategy' is just to be X (who goes first)
jokes.explanation: Good Fry stupidity - he thinks going first is strategic insight rather than just convention

jokes.text: Make it American. In individual plastic-wrapped slices.
jokes.setup: Leela offers fancy cheese to entice Nibbler to take medicine
jokes.punchline: His brain deterioration makes him prefer cheap processed cheese
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show Nibbler's intelligence declining - from sophisticated to the most processed option possible

jokes.text: I don't know, little Fuzzler.
jokes.setup: Nibbler asks what's happening to him in distress
jokes.punchline: Leela uses a childish nickname, highlighting his mental regression
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback that reinforces the sad reality of Nibbler's condition through Leela's treatment of him

jokes.text: Oh good, you're already crying.
jokes.setup: Professor enters with sad news
jokes.punchline: He's pleased they're already emotional, showing his lack of empathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - his complete lack of bedside manner delivered with maximum economy

jokes.text: It's from his ancestral pooping grounds.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Nibbler's litter is essential
jokes.punchline: Dignifying it as 'ancestral pooping grounds' instead of just saying bathroom area
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between the dignified 'ancestral' and crude 'pooping' - good absurdist humor

jokes.text: You must shrink down and battle the worms... face-to-face! Really? That sounds gross. Oh my, yes. And dangerous. Oh my, yes!
jokes.setup: Professor describes the mission requirements
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic confirmation of both gross and dangerous aspects
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - his glee at describing horrible conditions is very in-character

jokes.text: At least it's dead simple. That's where you're wrong!
jokes.setup: Amy tries to find a silver lining that it's simple
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately contradicts her optimism
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor behavior - can't let anyone have false hope, must correct them immediately

jokes.text: By reversing the lens, it becomes an all-new kind of ray that keeps things the same size.
jokes.setup: Professor explains his scientific breakthrough
jokes.punchline: The 'breakthrough' is a ray that does nothing - keeps things the same size
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - treating a non-functional device as an innovation with scientific enthusiasm

jokes.text: And this track? Track? Oh, right! It came with the set.
jokes.setup: Amy asks about equipment for the mission
jokes.punchline: Professor forgot the track entirely - it just came with the toy
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment showing his haphazard scientific method - important mission equipment is just toy accessories

jokes.text: Amy, shove it! No! You shove it! Oh. You mean this thing.
jokes.setup: Bender tells Amy to 'shove it' in what seems like an insult
jokes.punchline: Amy realizes he literally means shove the physical object
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke that plays on the dual meaning of 'shove it' - well-constructed

jokes.text: My Manwich!
jokes.setup: Something catastrophic happens during the shrinking process
jokes.punchline: Hermes' concern is for his sandwich getting destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes moment - his priorities during chaos, delivered with maximum economy

jokes.text: Maybe we can eat those weird pygmy hyena moles. Ow!
jokes.setup: Leela suggests eating the local wildlife for survival
jokes.punchline: She immediately gets bitten/attacked by one
jokes.explanation: Basic ironic timing - immediate consequences for her predatory comment

jokes.text: Orange sparklies? Zoidberg's going to bedazzle his lungs!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg inhales glittery particles
jokes.punchline: Amy describes it as 'bedazzling his lungs' - crafting term for medical danger
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting craft terminology to medical emergency - good visual humor

jokes.text: They're coming, why not. Who's coming? I don't know. Maybe those guys?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg warns that 'they' are coming
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know who, just guessing based on what he sees
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment - his dramatic warning is completely baseless and improvised

jokes.text: Hang on a sec! I'm taking a whiz. At least one of you has manners.
jokes.setup: Tense standoff with alien creatures
jokes.punchline: Fry stops to urinate, aliens appreciate his politeness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - inappropriate timing becomes polite behavior in alien context

jokes.text: We are beetles. Ah, of course. Dung beetles. It is pronounced Doong. The G is almost silent.
jokes.setup: Beetles identify themselves, Bender assumes they're dung beetles
jokes.punchline: Pretentious correction of pronunciation for 'dung'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - treating a crude word as if it needs sophisticated pronunciation, very quotable

jokes.text: Welcome to the world of Doon... g.
jokes.setup: Dramatic welcome to their world
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause to add the 'g' sound they emphasized
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the pronunciation gag with awkward timing

jokes.text: Could he be the Kwiznos Cadillach? Nope.
jokes.setup: Mystical prophecy about a messiah figure
jokes.punchline: The prophecy name sounds like restaurant and car brands, Fry dismisses immediately
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor mixing mystical prophecy with mundane corporate names, Fry's instant dismissal is perfect

jokes.text: The sun grows hot, and my deodorant grows weak.
jokes.setup: Dramatic statement about harsh desert conditions
jokes.punchline: Concern shifts to personal hygiene products failing
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between epic and mundane - treating deodorant failure as equally serious as environmental dangers

jokes.text: Can I offer anyone some spit? We have flat and sparkling.
jokes.setup: Hospitality offer for refreshments
jokes.punchline: The beverages are varieties of spit, described like water options
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out humor with sophisticated presentation - treating disgusting offerings like fine dining

jokes.text: That's what Zoidberg snorted. Does that make me cool? Absolutely not!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks if snorting the sacred substance makes him cool
jokes.punchline: Immediate, emphatic rejection of his coolness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his hopeful question met with brutal honesty, great economy of dialogue

jokes.text: Like Narlo here. Dude, I feel like I'm spinning...
jokes.setup: Example of someone who had a bad reaction to the glitter
jokes.punchline: Narlo is literally spinning/disoriented from the effects
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag showing the drug effects - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Not the cone of shame!
jokes.setup: Leela puts a cone on Nibbler to stop his behavior
jokes.punchline: Nibbler's dramatic reaction to the pet cone
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to popular pet trope - functional but relies on external reference for humor

jokes.text: I see the way. And I have a serious craving for frosting.
jokes.setup: Mystical vision provides important guidance
jokes.punchline: Also produces mundane craving for sweets
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between spiritual and material desires - mystical experience has petty side effects

jokes.text: Make a right at the large sand mound, then a left at the medium-sized mound. After the fifth mound, you'll see a mound. So many mounds!
jokes.setup: Detailed directions through the desert
jokes.punchline: All landmarks are just different sized mounds, Amy's exasperated reaction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the futility of desert navigation - builds absurdity well with repetition

jokes.text: One wrong mound means certain death. Or just take the roach-thopter.
jokes.setup: Dire warning about navigation dangers
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of much easier transportation option
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy - dramatic buildup deflated by simple alternative

jokes.text: Bilgar is our second-best guide. Our best guide got eaten by a sandworm.
jokes.setup: Introducing their guide Bilgar
jokes.punchline: He's only second-best because the best one died horribly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about professional competency - the casualness of mentioning death

jokes.text: Whatever you do, do not walk like a Doong beetle. Oops!
jokes.setup: Critical warning about not attracting the worms
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately does the forbidden action
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry moment - immediately violating instructions, predictable but in-character

jokes.text: Every living creature here is a total barf bag! Uh, n-no offense, Bilgar.
jokes.setup: Bender insults all the local wildlife
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly tries to exempt their guide from the insult
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - his tactlessness followed by half-hearted damage control

jokes.text: These little crawly guys are cute. Hello, friends. That's the worst one of all!
jokes.setup: Leela finds some creatures adorable
jokes.punchline: Guide reveals they're the most dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on the cute/deadly trope - well-timed revelation

jokes.text: Hey, you know my grandfather was a pounder. Hey, I guess that makes me a quarter-pounder.
jokes.setup: Fry mentions his grandfather's profession
jokes.punchline: Makes a McDonald's reference about being 1/4 of his grandfather
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Fry's tendency for random connections, though relies on brand reference

jokes.text: Or at least a substantial butt!? Sorry, guys, I'm a flatty.
jokes.setup: Leela criticizes Fry's inability to hold up his pants
jokes.punchline: Fry admits to having a flat butt
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Fry's matter-of-fact acceptance of his anatomical shortcoming

jokes.text: Hey! Don't bogart that glitter!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg wants more of the mystical substance
jokes.punchline: Uses drug slang 'bogart' (hog/monopolize) for the glitter
jokes.explanation: Clever use of drug slang for the mystical substance - shows Zoidberg's desperation for more

jokes.text: A tasty wienerschnitzel? A funny monkey? Sandstorm!
jokes.setup: Characters guess what's approaching
jokes.punchline: Their silly guesses contrasted with actual danger
jokes.explanation: Good character differentiation through their different silly guesses before reality hits

jokes.text: They call me the two-ton Fred Astaire. The jitterbug? The litter bug!
jokes.setup: Bender boasts about his dancing ability
jokes.punchline: Corrects 'jitterbug' to 'litter bug' for the litter box context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting dance terminology to their current situation, very Bender-like boasting

jokes.text: I know you. You're those worms that used to live in my tum-tum. Only you made me smarter. I'll take your word for it.
jokes.setup: Fry recognizes the worms from a previous episode
jokes.punchline: Worm's sarcastic response to Fry claiming they made him smarter
jokes.explanation: Good callback with skeptical response that undercuts Fry's claim about being made smarter

jokes.text: Good job... knot!
jokes.setup: Bender appears to compliment someone's performance
jokes.punchline: He's actually talking to a literal knot he's tied
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay with good misdirection - audience thinks he's praising a person

jokes.text: Look! Up in the sky! It's a bird! It's a turd! It's Nibbler!
jokes.setup: Classic Superman reference format
jokes.punchline: Substitutes 'turd' for 'plane' before revealing it's actually Nibbler
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that plays with famous cultural reference, 'turd' fits the litter box setting

jokes.text: How dumb are you on a scale of one to Fry? I can't even count that high anymore.
jokes.setup: Leela asks about Nibbler's intelligence using Fry as the low end
jokes.punchline: Nibbler says he can't count high enough to reach Fry's level of stupidity
jokes.explanation: Excellent multi-layered joke - insults both Fry's intelligence and Nibbler's current state, with perfect ironic structure

jokes.text: It shows him with blonde hair and blue eyes, but the rest is spot on.
jokes.setup: Ancient prophecy supposedly depicts Nibbler as messiah
jokes.punchline: The only matching features are hair and eye color (which Nibbler doesn't have)
jokes.explanation: Good visual irony - claiming accuracy while describing features the character obviously lacks

jokes.text: Really? 'Cause I'm seeing poop. Open your minds and see as I see.
jokes.setup: Nibbler waxes poetic about seeing beauty everywhere
jokes.punchline: Bender bluntly states he just sees poop, Nibbler persists
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Nibbler's spiritual awakening vs Bender's literal perspective

jokes.text: It's pronounced doong. Uh, sorry, Messiah. Keep going.
jokes.setup: Beetle corrects Nibbler's pronunciation during his spiritual speech
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly apologizes for interrupting the messiah
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates awkward interruption of profound moment with petty correction

jokes.text: And that in itself is blutier... Um, uh, boo-too-poo-pool. Pretty.
jokes.setup: Nibbler tries to express profound thoughts
jokes.punchline: His degraded brain can only produce nonsense syllables, which he finds beautiful
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny portrayal of cognitive decline - his appreciation of meaningless sounds is both sad and sweet

jokes.text: I can no longer predict the ending of an M. Night Shyamalan movie.
jokes.setup: Nibbler describes his mental deterioration
jokes.punchline: Uses inability to predict twist endings as measure of intelligence loss
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference that works as both intelligence test and commentary on predictable movie twists

jokes.text: Though my mental, uh, brain may deteriorate, I will always look back on what we had with... big happy. And you is... be my buddy and... so, so... itchy.
jokes.setup: Nibbler tries to express deep feelings for Leela
jokes.punchline: His speech degrades into childish language and random observations
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking and funny portrayal of cognitive decline - maintains emotional core while showing deterioration

jokes.text: You wanna blow your nose on my shirt?
jokes.setup: Fry tries to comfort crying Leela
jokes.punchline: His idea of comfort is offering his shirt as a tissue
jokes.explanation: Very Fry-like attempt at kindness that's simultaneously gross and sweet

jokes.text: I can smell sound. Fry's singing stinks up the whole apartment.
jokes.setup: Discussion of sensory scrambling effects
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg claims he can smell sound, specifically Fry's bad singing
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg joke playing on synesthesia while taking a shot at Fry's singing

jokes.text: ♪ I feel a song coming on! ♪
jokes.setup: Fry threatens to sing if they don't help Leela
jokes.punchline: He starts singing, proving Zoidberg's point about it stinking
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the previous joke about his singing, works as both threat and confirmation

jokes.text: Oh, that is gross, man!
jokes.setup: Discovery that parasitic worms have their own sub-parasites
jokes.punchline: The parasitic worm finds its own parasites disgusting
jokes.explanation: Great ironic humor - parasites being disgusted by smaller parasites on them

jokes.text: That's too many levels! We've gotta kill those mites!
jokes.setup: Discovery of parasites within parasites within parasites
jokes.punchline: Amy's exasperated reaction to the complexity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the absurd levels of plot complexity, Amy speaking for the audience

jokes.text: Bender, stop! It's me, Zoidberg! Sorry! I can't hear your screams over the tapping!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to stop Bender from attacking
jokes.punchline: Bender's tap dancing is too loud for him to hear screams for mercy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with very Bender-like priorities - dancing more important than mercy
